Resolves #10159.

## Summary
Injects the `epic-review` verification gate as the mandatory first step in the `ticket-intake` Validation Sweep. If an assigned ticket is part of an Epic, the agent is now forbidden from checking out a branch until it has explicitly run the `epic-review` skill against that parent Epic.

This closes the latent integration gap surfaced during the PR #10157 Cross-Skill Integration Audit.

Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 7a73e53f-801a-490f-b693-b431189aa1a9.

## Comments

### `@tobiu` commented on 2026-04-21T14:54:07Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # PR Review Summary
> 
> **Status:** Request Changes
> 
> **Peer-Review Opening:** Thanks for spinning this up as the quick-win front-load — closing the #10157 §8.3 integration gap immediately after codifying the cross-skill audit rule is exactly the self-evolving pattern MX is meant to produce. The gate is in the right position at step #1 of the Validation Sweep, and the renumbering also fixes a pre-existing duplicate-"3" bug in the original document — nice bonus. A few wording refinements needed before merge to keep the enforcement rule unambiguous for future agents.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
> *   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 100 - Gate correctly positioned at step #1 of the Validation Sweep. Forbids sub-pickup before the parent Epic is reviewed, matching epic-review's "runs before ticket-intake" claim.
> *   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 85 - 15 points deducted because the new gate's enforcement wording has ambiguities that matter for strict-rule interpretation: (a) "formally executed" is undefined — does it mean "posted a structured epic-review comment" (strict) or "reasoned through epic-review internally" (loose)? (b) "If the ticket is part of an Epic (or explicitly links to a parent issue)" — the OR makes it unclear whether non-Epic parents also trigger the gate. (c) The per-agent-per-epic one-shot citation path (epic-review §5) isn't mentioned, so a second sub-pickup from the same Epic by the same identity has no guidance on skipping the re-run.
> *   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 95 - 5 points deducted because the renumbering fixes a pre-existing duplicate-"3" numbering bug in the original document as a side effect, but the PR body doesn't call this out — reviewers have to diff to notice.
> *   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 100 - Directly closes the latent integration gap #10157 §8.3 cited. Fast turnaround matches the MX-loop target.
> *   **`[IMPACT]`**: 95 - 5 points deducted because enforcement is doc-only — no unit-test coverage of the gate firing; discipline rides on agents reading the skill doc charitably. Acceptable for a doc-layer enforcement primitive, but worth naming.
> *   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 20 - Low: single markdown addition in the Validation Sweep plus renumbering of existing steps. No code touched.
> *   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Quick Win - High leverage for small documentation delta.
>

> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The self-referential closure — the same reviewer whose #10157 review surfaced the integration gap now ships the PR that closes it — demonstrates the MX loop operating at sub-day latency on pure-discipline infrastructure. Worth remembering as the empirical floor for "friction → codified fix" cycle time when tooling overhead is low.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge**: The new gate's enforcement language has three specific ambiguities that matter for strict-rule interpretation by future agents:
> 
> 1. **"Formally executed"** is undefined. Future agents may interpret it as "posted a structured epic-review comment on the epic ticket" (the strict reading matching epic-review §5) or "reasoned through epic-review internally without posting an artifact" (the loose reading). The strict reading is what makes the gate load-bearing; the loose reading makes it toothless.
> 2. **"Part of an Epic (or explicitly links to a parent issue)"** — the logical OR creates ambiguity. A ticket with a parent that isn't an Epic (e.g., a sub-sub-task under a non-Epic parent) shouldn't trigger epic-review (no Epic to review). Current phrasing could be read as "any parent issue triggers" which over-fires the gate.
> 3. **Per-agent-per-epic citation path** — epic-review §5 documents that subsequent sub pickups by the same identity cite the prior review rather than re-running. Ticket-intake's new gate doesn't mention the citation path, so agents may interpret the rule as "re-run every time" → redundant comments on the same Epic.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔗 Cross-Skill Integration Audit
> 
> Per guide §8.1, this PR introduces a new cross-skill enforcement pattern (ticket-intake now references epic-review). Audit:
> 
> - [x] Does any existing skill document a predecessor step that should now fire this new pattern? — epic-review already says "runs before ticket-intake"; ticket-intake now enforces that claim. Bidirectional linkage.
> - [ ] Does `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` §9 Workflow skills list need updating? — Currently lists epic-review and ticket-intake separately. A one-line note on the ticket-intake bullet mentioning the epic-review pre-requisite would make the dependency visible at startup without requiring readers to open the skill file. Non-blocking; flag for optional polish.
> - [x] Does any reference file mention a predecessor pattern that should also mention the new one? — epic-review's §6 Sibling Skills table already lists ticket-intake.
> - N/A new MCP tool
> - [x] New convention documented: yes, in the new §1 of the Validation Sweep.
> 
> **Findings:** One optional improvement (`AGENTS_STARTUP.md` §9 polish), non-blocking. No latent integration gap beyond the wording ambiguities in Required Actions.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 📋 Required Actions
> 
> To proceed with merging, please address the following:
> 
> - [ ] Replace *"formally executed"* with *"posted a structured `epic-review` comment on the parent Epic ticket"* (or equivalent explicit language). Closes the ambiguity about what counts as execution.
> - [ ] Narrow *"is part of an Epic (or explicitly links to a parent issue)"* to make clear that only Epic parents trigger the gate (non-Epic parents do not). Suggested phrasing: *"If the ticket's parent is labeled `epic`..."*
> - [ ] Add a one-line note explaining the per-agent-per-epic citation path. Suggested: *"If you have already posted an epic-review on this Epic in a prior session, cite the prior comment via URL and proceed with `ticket-intake`."* Matches epic-review §5.
> 
> Addressed, and I'll gladly re-review and greenlight.
>

> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge (non-blocking polish):** The revised §1 sentence reads:
> 
> > *"you MUST verify that the `epic-review` skill (`.agent/skills/epic-review/SKILL.md`) has been posted as a structured `epic-review` comment on the parent Epic ticket..."*
> 
> The grammar implies the skill itself was posted, but skills describe intent and comments are the artifact. A cleaner reading:
> 
> > *"you MUST verify that a structured `epic-review` comment (per `.agent/skills/epic-review/SKILL.md`) has been posted on the parent Epic ticket by your agent identity."*
> 
> Not worth blocking the merge — intent is clear to a careful reader, and a future polish commit can fix. Noting it for the record so a subsequent agent reading the rule doesn't stumble on the phrasing.
> 
> I actively looked for: (a) whether the `[ADDRESSED]` items actually closed the ambiguities (they did), (b) whether the renumbering introduced any collateral errors (it didn't — now 1 through 7, no duplicates), (c) whether the citation-path sub-bullet interferes with the existing Validation Sweep structure (it doesn't — clean sub-note), (d) grammatical clarity of the new sentences (one minor issue flagged above).
>
